message:
fix(ft-kernel-cpu): match torch.sign on signed zeros and NaN (frankentorch-wfyq)

sign_scalar / sign_tensor_contiguous_f64 / sign_tensor_contiguous_f32
all routed through Rust's f64::signum / f32::signum, which return
±1.0 for ±0.0 (IEEE 754 sign-bit semantics). torch.sign instead maps
both signed zeros to +0.0, propagates NaN, and returns ±1.0 only for
non-zero finite values.

Replaced the .signum calls with new torch_sign_f64 / torch_sign_f32
helpers. The kernel-cpu sign tests previously documented the Rust
behavior as expected ("Rust signum: +0.0 → 1.0, -0.0 → -1.0") — those
are now updated to assert the torch parity and extended to cover both
signed zeros + NaN. Two stale ft-autograd tests
(scalar_sign_forward_zero, tensor_sign_forward) also updated.

Tests:
- ft-kernel-cpu: sign_scalar_returns_expected_value (extended),
  sign_scalar_propagates_nan (new),
  sign_tensor_contiguous_returns_expected_values (extended),
  sign_tensor_contiguous_propagates_nan (new).
- ft-autograd: scalar_sign_forward_zero (covers +0.0 and -0.0 now),
  tensor_sign_forward (covers both signed zeros + non-zero negatives).

ft-kernel-cpu 304 + ft-autograd 314 + ft-api 1086 tests pass; clippy +
ubs clean.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -146,6 +146,7 @@
 {"id":"frankentorch-vws","title":"Validate performance SLOs from spec against actual measurements","description":"COMPREHENSIVE_SPEC_FOR_FRANKENTORCH_V1.md §17 defines 8 performance budgets that have NEVER been measured:

| Path | Workload | Budget |
|---|---|---|
| elementwise forward | 100M elements | p95 <= 200 ms |
| backward pass | representative autograd graphs | p95 <= 1.35x forward baseline |
| matmul core | medium dense matrices | p95 <= 240 ms |
| optimizer step | 10M params Adam | p95 <= 130 ms |
| checkpoint save/load | 2 GB model state | throughput >= 350 MB/s |
| memory footprint | training step profile | peak RSS regression <= +8% |
| allocation churn | backward-heavy trace | alloc count regression <= +10% |
| tail stability | benchmark families | p99 regression <= +7% |

The spec's M4 milestone (Hardening) requires performance gates to pass. Currently we have exactly one benchmark (scalar DAC microbench, p95=4698ns) which doesn't cover any of the 8 SLOs.

Implementation plan:
1. Create a benchmark binary in ft-conformance (or a new crate) that runs each of the 8 workloads
2. For each: run 100 iterations, compute p50/p95/p99, compare against the budget
3. Emit structured JSON results to artifacts/phase2c/performance/
4. Create a gate script that reads the JSON and fails if any SLO is exceeded
5. Document whether FrankenTorch meets, exceeds, or fails each budget

Key consideration: We're CPU-only and pure Rust with no BLAS — we may not meet matmul or elementwise budgets for 100M elements. That's OK — the goal is to MEASURE and know where we stand, then document realistic budgets.

Tests:
- Benchmark binary should be runnable via: rch exec -- cargo run -p ft-conformance --bin run_perf_slos
- JSON output should be machine-readable and diffable
- Gate script should exit 0 (pass) or 1 (fail) with human-readable report","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-07T04:17:24.426519762Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T20:53:59.370070209Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T20:53:59.369819234Z","close_reason":"Implemented perf-SLO runner/gate, validated smoke path, and captured a verified single-sample full-profile measurement with documented direct-budget failures plus baseline-required regression budgets; remaining baseline/CI handoff moved to frankentorch-3cp.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

+{"id":"frankentorch-wfyq","title":"sign returns -1.0 for -0.0, diverges from torch.sign","description":"sign_scalar and sign_tensor_contiguous_f64 use Rust's f64::signum which returns -1.0 for -0.0 and 1.0 for 0.0 (sign-bit semantics). torch.sign returns 0.0 for both signed zeros. Existing tests at crates/ft-kernel-cpu/src/lib.rs:9188-9206 document the Rust behavior as expected. Fix: replace .signum() with explicit branches that map both signed zeros to 0.0, propagate NaN, and return ±1.0 only for non-zero finite values. Update tests to assert torch parity.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-28T21:23:16.000385332Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-28T21:26:22.951035096Z","closed_at":"2026-04-28T21:26:22.949171877Z","close_reason":"sign now matches torch.sign: both signed zeros map to +0.0, NaN propagates as NaN, and ±non-zero return ±1.0. F64 (sign_scalar/sign_tensor_contiguous_f64) and F32 (sign_tensor_contiguous_f32) all routed through new torch_sign_{f32,f64} helpers replacing Rust's IEEE-754-sign-bit signum. 4 new edge-case tests in ft-kernel-cpu + 2 stale ft-autograd tests fixed; ft-kernel-cpu 304 + ft-autograd 314 + ft-api 1086 tests pass; clippy + ubs clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"frankentorch-wze","title":"Escalate blocked_issues_cache import-path page leak and preserve safe tracker workflow","description":"Follow-up to frankentorch-xsp.

Investigation isolated the integrity regression to the import path: explicit `br sync --import-only` and any command that auto-imports current JSONL into a clean vacuumed DB. The stable signature is blocked_issues_cache churn: old blocked-cache table/index pages become `PRAGMA integrity_check` `never used` pages after import/rebuild.

Safe on a clean vacuumed DB: `br info`, `br doctor`, `br sync --status`, and commands with `--no-auto-import`.

Artifacts:
- artifacts/beads_recovery/FRANKENTORCH_XSP_IMPORT_PATH_REPORT_20260407.md
- artifacts/beads_recovery/xsp_probe_20260407T025948Z/results.json
- artifacts/beads_recovery/xsp_import_focus_20260407T030054Z/results.json
- artifacts/beads_recovery/xsp_repeat_import_20260407T030112Z/results.json
- artifacts/beads_recovery/xsp_sequence_20260407T030136Z/results.json

Next work:
1. Escalate the import-path / blocked-cache page leak upstream in beads_rust or frankensqlite.
2. Preserve a local recovery playbook that warns agents to avoid auto-import on freshly vacuumed recovery DBs until the upstream fix lands.
3. Re-validate the workaround after the upstream fix by repeating the xsp probe matrix.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-07T03:04:23.139752494Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-08T19:11:33.555177457Z","closed_at":"2026-04-08T19:11:33.554917796Z","close_reason":"Filed upstream escalation as beads_rust#224, expanded the archived import-path report with a concrete local recovery playbook, and added a temporary AGENTS.md warning to keep vacuumed recovery DB sessions on --no-auto-import until the fix lands.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
